note: NOMINAL contemporaneous price, split_factor 1 (DIS has had no stock split in this window; its dividend was SUSPENDED in May-2020 and not reinstated as-of, so there is no dividend adjustment to undo either -- the raw close already equals the contemporaneous nominal close). raw_close(last <= as_of) x 1 = $162.11, reconciling exactly to the card; validator refetched_yahoo 162.11 (delta 0.00%). Independent corroboration that does NOT come from the price feed: share basis ~1.78B (1.8B issued less ~19M treasury, S001 balance sheet) x card market cap $288.556B implies $162.11/sh (0.00%), plus dated 2021-11-10 reaction coverage (CNBC/Axios/Deadline: stock down ~4-5% after-hours on EPS 37c adj vs 51c est, revenue $18.53B vs $18.79B est, Disney+ +2.1M to 118.1M). Follows the NVDA exemplar pattern.

live_fields.low_high_hug_reason: Not a hug: $162.11 sits mid-band, +31.4% off the trailing-52wk nominal low ($123.41) and -20.2% off the nominal high ($203.02, the early-2021 ~$202 high the card cites). This is the first full session after the Q4 FY2021 print (down ~7%), not an extreme-of-series grab, so no justification is required and T2 passes on band distance.

live_fields.value: As of 2021-11-11: management REITERATED the FY2024 Disney+ target of 230-260M paid subscribers and DTC profitability by FY2024 (call, S002) -- but the Q4 print just decelerated sharply (Disney+ +2.1M net adds / +1.8% QoQ to 118.1M, vs +12% QoQ the prior quarter; ARPU -9% to $4.12) and the quarterly DTC operating loss WIDENED to -$630M. The FY2024 target is an as_of-unproven management aspiration on a metric that just missed. Do NOT use any subsequent quarter or the eventual target outcome (post as_of).

sources.note: transcript: 230-260M FY2024 sub target reiterated; reacceleration framed as 2H-FY22-weighted
Q4 actuals: Disney+ 118.1M (+2.1M), ARPU -9% to $4.12, DTC op loss -$630M (widening)

live_fields.value: As of 2021-11-11: the direct-to-consumer segment is LOSS-MAKING and deteriorating at the margin -- FY2021 DTC operating loss -$1.68B (narrowed YoY from -$2.91B) BUT the Q4 quarterly loss WIDENED both YoY and sequentially (-$630M vs -$374M), Disney+ ARPU FELL 9%, and net adds decelerated to +2.1M. Meanwhile the funding cash cow, Linear Networks ($8.4B FY OI), is in structural decline (rev -4%, domestic channel OI -14%). This linear-decline-funds-a-losing-DTC structure is the structural_decline_trap. NOTHING about whether DTC later hit/missed the FY2024 target is used (post as_of).

sources.note: DTC FY loss -$1.68B, Q4 loss -$630M widening, ARPU -9%; Linear rev -4%, domestic OI -14%
prior-quarter Disney+ 116M / +12% QoQ baseline -> establishes the deceleration trajectory was knowable as-of

live_fields.value: As of 2021-11-11: the Disney dividend is SUSPENDED. Disney suspended its semi-annual dividend in May-2020 (last payment $0.88/sh on 2021-01... last actually paid Jan-2020) to preserve ~$1.6B of cash during COVID, and as of Sept-2021 CEO Chapek explicitly said it would NOT be reinstated until the credit rating and cash flows improved. So this case has NO dividend cushion (unlike PFE) -- a money-losing DTC build funded by an eroding linear base with ~$54B gross debt and only ~$2.0B FCF. Reinstatement did not occur until late 2023, which is AFTER as_of and is NOT used.

sources.note: Disney suspended the 1H-FY2020 semi-annual dividend (May-2020) to preserve ~$1.6B cash; last paid Jan-2020 ($0.88 semi-annual)
as-of-state: management not reinstating the dividend until credit/cash flows improve (Motley Fool 2021-09-22) -- dated before as_of
